title: Intensely Smoky Polish Meat Skillet
description: A hearty, one-pot Polish dish featuring smoky meat, chewy texture, and a crispy breadcrumb topping, all made in a keto-friendly style. This dish is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, cook the ground pork over medium-high heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until it is translucent.
In a bowl, mix together the breadcrumbs and shredded cheddar cheese.
Push the meat mixture to one side of the skillet, then add the breadcrumb mixture to the other side.
Fold the meat mixture over the breadcrumb mixture to combine.
Make two wells in the meat mixture and crack an egg into each well.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 20 minutes, or until the eggs are cooked to your desired doneness.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
